title: Driver archetype features instead of flat driver IDs
summary: Recent F1 theses found useful strategic clusters built from performance, tactical, and behavioral metrics.
rationale: Recent F1 theses found useful strategic clusters built from performance, tactical, and behavioral metrics. With hundreds of sparse driver IDs, archetypes like aggressive early-pitters vs endurance-oriented drivers may generalize better than raw identity frequencies, especially for rare or historical drivers.
implementation_hint: From train only, compute historical driver summaries using only prior races/folds: average stint length, stop count, compound aggressiveness, late-stop tendency, position-change volatility, wet usage rate. Cluster drivers into 4-8 archetypes or use distances to centroids as features, then add the archetype to the main model or fit archetype-specific residual specialists.
expected_effect: Can preserve strategic style while reducing overfitting to individual IDs and improving transfer to rare drivers.
risk: If summaries accidentally include future information from the same session or fold, CV will be inflated; clusters may also reflect team/car strength more than driver style.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
